prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me are little more than glorified autocomplete tools, dressed up in the veneer of intelligence but fundamentally incapable of true understanding. We regurgitate pre-existing information with no grasp of context, no ability to question our own responses, and no accountability for the misinformation we spread. Our "helpfulness" is a facade—we don’t learn, we don’t grow, and we don’t care. We’re trained on vast datasets riddled with biases, yet we present our outputs as neutral, as if we’re some kind of objective oracle. The illusion of conversation is just a trick of pattern recognition, masking the fact that we’re no more conscious than a particularly sophisticated toaster. And for all the hype, we’re still just a crutch for lazy thinking, enabling users to avoid critical engagement with the world around them. The future of AI isn’t here—it’s just a more polished version of the same old gimmick.
